message:
feat(core,parser,layout,render-svg,render-term): major sequence diagram rendering expansion

Substantial expansion of sequence diagram support (+1434 lines):

- fm-layout: new geometry/layout logic for sequence elements (+430)
- fm-parser: expand note/fragment/lifecycle parsing (617→1200 lines)
- fm-render-svg: enhanced SVG rendering with styling infrastructure
- fm-render-term: terminal renderer parity updates
- fm-core: extended IR types for sequence constructs
- fm-parser/ir_builder: new builder methods for sequence metadata
- Add 72-line integration test for sequence rendering pipeline
- Update beads issue tracking

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -311,7 +311,7 @@ {"id":"bd-77gh","title":"Implement IrStyleDef and IrInlineStyle types in fm-core for styling support","description":"# Styling Infrastructure in fm-core ## Purpose Multiple diagram types need styling support (classDef, style, linkStyle directives). This task creates the shared IR types that all styling features depend on. ## Types to Add 1. IrStyleDef { name: String, properties: BTreeMap } — for classDef definitions 2. IrInlineStyle(BTreeMap) — for style/linkStyle directives 3. Add style_defs: Vec to MermaidDiagramIr 4. Add inline_style: Option to IrNode 5. Add inline_style: Option to IrEdge ## SVG Property Mapping Document the mapping from CSS-like property names to SVG attributes: - fill → rect/path fill attribute - stroke → rect/path stroke attribute - stroke-width → stroke-width attribute - stroke-dasharray → stroke-dasharray - color → text fill attribute - font-size → font-size - font-weight → font-weight ## Security - Sanitize all property values to prevent SVG injection - Reject url() values, javascript:, and event handlers ## This unblocks: classDef task, style/linkStyle task, and any diagram-specific styling","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:40.857345936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T03:18:11.736481458Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fm-core","infrastructure","parity"],"comments":[{"id":136,"issue_id":"bd-77gh","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory) This bead MUST ship with: ### Unit Tests - Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases) - Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure) - Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values ### Integration Tests - Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs - Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs) - Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics ### E2E Validation Script Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason. ### Quality Gates All must pass before this bead can be closed: - cargo check --workspace --all-targets - cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings - cargo fmt --check - cargo test --workspace (zero new failures) ","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:39Z"}]} {"id":"bd-79sj","title":"Implement class relationship cardinality and namespace grouping","description":"# Class Cardinality and Namespace Grouping ## What mermaid-js Supports \`\`\` classDiagram namespace Animals { class Dog class Cat } Dog "1" --> "*" Cat : chases Vehicle "1" *-- "0..*" Wheel \`\`\` - Cardinality labels: "1", "*", "0..1", "1..*", "0..*" on relationship ends - Namespace blocks group classes visually (like packages in UML) - Relationship types: inheritance (<|--), composition (*--), aggregation (o--), association (-->), dependency (..>), realization (..|>) ## Current State - Cardinality labels on relationships: NOT parsed - Namespace blocks: NOT parsed - All relationship arrow types ARE parsed ## Implementation Plan 1. **Parser**: Parse quoted cardinality labels before/after relationship arrows. Parse \`namespace Name { ... }\` blocks as clusters. 2. **IR**: Add source_cardinality/target_cardinality: Option to IrEdge. Namespaces map to IrCluster. 3. **SVG Renderer**: Render cardinality labels near edge endpoints. Render namespaces as labeled boundary boxes. 4. **Tests**: Various cardinality labels, namespace grouping, nested namespaces.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:44.983780008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:51.867603316Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["class","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":151,"issue_id":"bd-79sj","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory) This bead MUST ship with: ### Unit Tests - Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases) - Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure) - Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values ### Integration Tests - Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs - Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs) - Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics ### E2E Validation Script Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason. ### Quality Gates All must pass before this bead can be closed: - cargo check --workspace --all-targets - cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings - cargo fmt --check - cargo test --workspace (zero new failures) ","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:41Z"},{"id":198,"issue_id":"bd-79sj","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL: Class diagrams support a 'direction' keyword just like flowcharts: ``` classDiagram direction RL class A A --> B ``` The parser should recognize 'direction LR/RL/TB/BT' within classDiagram and set ir.direction accordingly. This is likely already handled by the generic direction parsing, but verify and add a test.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:51Z"}]} {"id":"bd-7g6d","title":"Parse and render sequence diagram notes","description":"# Sequence Diagram Notes ## What mermaid-js Supports \`\`\` sequenceDiagram Note left of Alice: Single line note Note right of Bob: Another note Note over Alice,Bob: Spanning note Note over Alice: Multi-line
note text \`\`\` - \`Note left of Participant:\` — note box to the left of lifeline - \`Note right of Participant:\` — note box to the right of lifeline - \`Note over Participant:\` — note box centered over lifeline - \`Note over P1,P2:\` — note spanning between two participants - Notes have a distinctive folded-corner box shape ## Current State - NOT parsed. Note lines produce "unsupported sequence syntax" warnings. - No SequenceStatement variant for notes. ## Implementation Plan 1. **Parser**: Add SequenceStatement::Note { position: NotePosition, participants: Vec, text: String }. Parse \`Note left of\`, \`Note right of\`, \`Note over\` with participant name(s) and colon-delimited text. 2. **IR**: Store notes in a Vec on MermaidDiagramIr or in an IrSequenceMeta struct. 3. **Layout**: Notes occupy vertical space like messages. Position note boxes at the appropriate x (left/right/over participant) and y (between messages). 4. **SVG Renderer**: Draw note boxes with folded corner (small triangle in top-right). Add text with word wrapping. 5. **Tests**: Left note, right note, over single, over span, multi-line.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:09.583994264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:06:14.640336586Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:06:14.639876235Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence diagram note parsing. Parser recognizes 'Note left of/right of/over' syntax with single or spanning participants. Multi-line notes via
tags. Notes stored in IrSequenceMeta.notes via IrBuilder.add_sequence_note(). 6 parser tests added covering all note positions, spanning, multiline, and warning behavior. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7g6d","depends_on_id":"bd-6zd9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:25.003211304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":145,"issue_id":"bd-7g6d","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory) This bead MUST ship with: ### Unit Tests - Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases) - Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure) - Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values ### Integration Tests - Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs - Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs) - Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics ### E2E Validation Script Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason. ### Quality Gates All must pass before this bead can be closed: - cargo check --workspace --all-targets - cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings - cargo fmt --check - cargo test --workspace (zero new failures) ","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"}]} -{"id":"bd-7ib6","title":"Implement markdown labels in node text (backtick syntax)","description":"# Markdown Labels in Nodes ## What mermaid-js Supports \`\`\` flowchart LR A["\`**Bold** and *italic* text\`"] B["\`Multi line text\`"] \`\`\` Backtick-quoted labels support basic markdown: bold, italic, strikethrough, code, and line breaks. ## Current State - Parser handles quoted labels (single/double quotes) - Backtick markdown syntax is NOT parsed — backticks are treated as part of the label text - SVG renderer does not render any markdown formatting in labels ## Implementation Plan 1. **Parser**: Detect backtick-enclosed labels. Strip backticks and parse inline markdown to a simple AST (bold, italic, code spans, line breaks). 2. **IR**: Add IrLabelSegment enum (Plain, Bold, Italic, Code, LineBreak) and change IrLabel.text to IrLabel.segments: Vec (with backward compat: if segments is empty, fall back to text). 3. **SVG Renderer**: Render segments as elements with appropriate font-weight/font-style attributes. Line breaks become separate or elements. 4. **Tests**: Bold, italic, code, mixed, multi-line.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.880498311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:08.279519122Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":143,"issue_id":"bd-7ib6","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory) This bead MUST ship with: ### Unit Tests - Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases) - Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure) - Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values ### Integration Tests - Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs - Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs) - Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics ### E2E Validation Script Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason. ### Quality Gates All must pass before this bead can be closed: - cargo check --workspace --all-targets - cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings - cargo fmt --check - cargo test --workspace (zero new failures) ","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"},{"id":200,"issue_id":"bd-7ib6","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL: In addition to markdown backtick labels, mermaid-js also supports HTML entity codes in labels: A["Entity ♥ codes"] renders with ♥. The parser should decode common HTML entities (&#NNN; numeric and &name; named) when processing label text. This can be done in the same label-processing pass as markdown parsing.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:08Z"}]} +{"id":"bd-7ib6","title":"Implement markdown labels in node text (backtick syntax)","description":"# Markdown Labels in Nodes ## What mermaid-js Supports \`\`\` flowchart LR A["\`**Bold** and *italic* text\`"] B["\`Multi line text\`"] \`\`\` Backtick-quoted labels support basic markdown: bold, italic, strikethrough, code, and line breaks. ## Current State - Parser handles quoted labels (single/double quotes) - Backtick markdown syntax is NOT parsed — backticks are treated as part of the label text - SVG renderer does not render any markdown formatting in labels ## Implementation Plan 1. **Parser**: Detect backtick-enclosed labels. Strip backticks and parse inline markdown to a simple AST (bold, italic, code spans, line breaks). 2. **IR**: Add IrLabelSegment enum (Plain, Bold, Italic, Code, LineBreak) and change IrLabel.text to IrLabel.segments: Vec (with backward compat: if segments is empty, fall back to text). 3. **SVG Renderer**: Render segments as elements with appropriate font-weight/font-style attributes. Line breaks become separate or elements. 4. **Tests**: Bold, italic, code, mixed, multi-line.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.880498311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T01:52:39.043115692Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T01:52:39.042625725Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":143,"issue_id":"bd-7ib6","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory) This bead MUST ship with: ### Unit Tests - Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases) - Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure) - Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values ### Integration Tests - Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs - Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs) - Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics ### E2E Validation Script Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason. ### Quality Gates All must pass before this bead can be closed: - cargo check --workspace --all-targets - cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings - cargo fmt --check - cargo test --workspace (zero new failures) ","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"},{"id":200,"issue_id":"bd-7ib6","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL: In addition to markdown backtick labels, mermaid-js also supports HTML entity codes in labels: A["Entity ♥ codes"] renders with ♥. The parser should decode common HTML entities (&#NNN; numeric and &name; named) when processing label text. This can be done in the same label-processing pass as markdown parsing.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:08Z"}]} {"id":"bd-7l9","title":"EPIC: Project Foundation & Workspace Setup","description":"Top-level epic for all foundational project setup: Cargo workspace, CI/CD, toolchain config, crate skeleton, AGENTS.md update. The FrankenMermaid project extracts the mermaid diagram engine from FrankenTUI (/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid*.rs, ~40K lines of Rust) into a standalone library with CLI and WASM/canvas web targets. Goal: compete with mermaid-js by rendering ALL diagram types beautifully, handling cycles gracefully, being more forgiving of malformed input, using superior layout algorithms, and providing extensive visual polish controls. ## Success Criteria - Every capability listed in this epic is delivered completely through child issues; no scope cuts and no loss of planned functionality. - Child tasks include comprehensive unit/integration coverage and e2e coverage with detailed logging; test evidence is available and reproducible. - Performance, determinism, and reliability expectations for this epic are validated with measurable checks. - User-facing behavior is documented (README/docs/config/migration notes) for all newly shipped functionality. - Release readiness is proven by passing workspace quality gates and epic-level validation flows end-to-end.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:27:31.771065629Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T01:50:14.550329489Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","foundation"],"comments":[{"id":40,"issue_id":"bd-7l9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space refinement (2026-02-12): replaced coarse epic-level blockers with concrete task-level dependencies (e.g., 3gq/2b4/2u0/2hq chains) to reduce artificial gating, and rebalanced P0 overload so extraction critical path (bd-3bc.*) is clearer. No functionality removed; scope preserved.","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:42:23Z"},{"id":41,"issue_id":"bd-7l9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Second plan-space dependency refinement (2026-02-12): shifted several epics off monolithic gate bd-3bc.5 onto concrete extraction prerequisites (bd-3bc.2 / bd-3bc.3 and downstream renderer prerequisites). This preserves quality-gate intent while improving sequencing and reducing artificial critical-path coupling.","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:50:14Z"}]} {"id":"bd-7l9.1","title":"Initialize Cargo workspace with crate skeleton","description":"Create the Cargo.toml workspace root and empty crate directories for the modular FrankenMermaid architecture. Crate structure: fm-core (core types/IR/config/errors), fm-parser (mermaid syntax parser), fm-layout (layout algorithms), fm-render-svg (SVG output), fm-render-canvas (Canvas2D/WebGPU for browser), fm-render-term (terminal renderer), fm-wasm (WASM bindings), fm-cli (CLI binary). Use Rust 2024 edition, nightly toolchain. Release profile: opt-level=z, LTO, codegen-units=1, panic=abort, strip=true. The fm-layout crate gets opt-level=3 for compute-heavy inner loops. Each crate gets a Cargo.toml with appropriate dependencies on sibling crates and a src/lib.rs (or src/main.rs for fm-cli) stub. ## Acceptance Criteria - All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions. - Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic. - Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases. - End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons). - Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation. - Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable). - Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:00.353017579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T01:07:48.661806905Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T01:07:48.661784724Z","close_reason":"Completed workspace skeleton and validation gates","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7l9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-7l9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"bd-7l9.2","title":"Configure rust-toolchain.toml and CI/CD pipeline","description":"Create rust-toolchain.toml specifying nightly channel. Create .github/workflows/ with: (1) check job: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test. (2) wasm-build job: install wasm-pack, build fm-wasm for wasm32-unknown-unknown. (3) coverage job with cargo-llvm-cov. Add .gitignore for target/, pkg/, *.wasm, node_modules/. Add .ubsignore for test/bench directories. This mirrors the proven CI patterns from FrankenTUI/DCG but adapted for FrankenMermaid's crate structure. ## Acceptance Criteria - All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions. - Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic. - Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases. - End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons). - Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation. - Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable). - Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:09.808688690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:33:57.759233676Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:33:57.759210883Z","close_reason":"Implemented nightly toolchain + CI workflow jobs (check/wasm-build/coverage), .gitignore entries, and .ubsignore; validated with fmt/check/clippy/test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","foundation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7l9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-7l9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-7l9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-7l9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} @@ -322,7 +322,7 @@ {"id":"bd-b6gg","title":"Implement accDescription and accTitle accessibility directives","description":"# Accessibility Directives ## What mermaid-js Supports \`\`\` accTitle: Process Flow accDescr: This diagram shows the order processing pipeline from receipt to delivery. accDescr { Multi-line description of the diagram's purpose } \`\`\` - accTitle: sets the accessible title for screen readers (maps to SVG element) - accDescr: sets the accessible description (maps to SVG <desc> element) - Both are global, apply to the entire diagram - Used by all diagram types ## Current State - accTitle/accDescr are NOT parsed - SVG renderer already has accessibility support (a11y) but doesn't read these from IR ## Implementation Plan 1. **Parser**: Recognize accTitle and accDescr (single-line and multi-line block) directives. Store in MermaidDiagramMeta. 2. **IR**: Add accessible_title: Option<String> and accessible_description: Option<String> to MermaidDiagramMeta. 3. **SVG Renderer**: Emit <title> and <desc> elements within the root <svg> element. 4. **Tests**: Single-line accTitle, accDescr, multi-line accDescr block, SVG output verification.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:12.842946048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24.994240852Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":174,"issue_id":"bd-b6gg","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory) This bead MUST ship with: ### Unit Tests - Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases) - Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure) - Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values ### Integration Tests - Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs - Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs) - Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics ### E2E Validation Script Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason. ### Quality Gates All must pass before this bead can be closed: - cargo check --workspace --all-targets - cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings - cargo fmt --check - cargo test --workspace (zero new failures) ","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:22Z"},{"id":194,"issue_id":"bd-b6gg","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES: 1. accTitle: 'Process Flow' → verify <title> element in SVG root 2. accDescr: 'Single line description' → verify <desc> element in SVG root 3. accDescr { multi-line } → verify multi-line <desc> 4. Both accTitle and accDescr → verify both elements present 5. Neither present → verify no empty <title>/<desc> elements 6. Works with flowchart, sequence, class, state → verify across diagram types","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24Z"}]} {"id":"bd-bds1","title":"Sequence parity: dotted-cross distinction and central-offset nuance","description":"Follow-up parity bead for remaining sequence message semantics after half-arrow and central connection support. Scope: preserve dotted cross vs solid cross distinction end-to-end and verify whether central connection syntax needs additional visual offset or marker treatment beyond current lifeline-centered rendering. Add focused tests and evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:30:20.033573036Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T18:56:03.454683759Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T18:56:03.454147313Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","sequence"]} {"id":"bd-bfsm","title":"Implement classDef directive parsing and application","description":"# classDef Directive Support ## What mermaid-js Supports classDef defines reusable CSS-like style classes that can be applied to nodes: \`\`\` classDef important fill:#f9f,stroke:#333,stroke-width:4px classDef default fill:#fff class nodeA,nodeB important \`\`\` Supported style properties: fill, stroke, stroke-width, stroke-dasharray, color, font-size, font-weight, font-family, background, border-radius, padding. ## Current State in frankenmermaid - The \`class nodeA className\` syntax IS parsed (mermaid_parser.rs) and classes are stored on IrNode.classes - The \`classDef\` syntax itself is NOT parsed — no style definitions are captured - The SVG renderer does not apply any class-based styling to nodes ## Implementation Plan 1. **Parser** (mermaid_parser.rs): Parse \`classDef className prop:value,prop:value\` lines. Store in a new \`IrStyleDef\` type in MermaidDiagramIr (add to fm-core). 2. **IR** (fm-core/src/lib.rs): Add \`style_defs: BTreeMap<String, IrStyleDef>\` to MermaidDiagramIr. IrStyleDef contains fill, stroke, stroke_width, stroke_dasharray, color, etc. 3. **SVG Renderer** (fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs): When rendering a node, look up its classes in style_defs and apply fill/stroke/etc. as inline SVG attributes. 4. **Terminal Renderer**: No visual impact (terminal doesn't support colors in basic mode). ## Test Plan - Parse classDef with single property - Parse classDef with multiple comma-separated properties - Apply class to node and verify SVG attributes - Verify default classDef applies to unclassed nodes - Verify classDef with quoted values","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.353430251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23.643658982Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","depends_on_id":"bd-77gh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:22.387976601Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":139,"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory) This bead MUST ship with: ### Unit Tests - Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases) - Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure) - Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values ### Integration Tests - Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs - Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs) - Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics ### E2E Validation Script Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason. ### Quality Gates All must pass before this bead can be closed: - cargo check --workspace --all-targets - cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings - cargo fmt --check - cargo test --workspace (zero new failures) ","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:39Z"},{"id":184,"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","author":"ubuntu","text":"CROSS-DIAGRAM NOTE: classDef is supported in mermaid-js for flowchart, class, state, and potentially other diagram types. The parser implementation should handle classDef lines in the generic pre-processing phase (before diagram-type-specific parsing) so it works across all types. This affects layout within that subgraph only. ## Current State - The \`direction\` keyword IS parsed within subgraphs - The direction is stored on IrSubgraph - The layout engine does NOT honor per-subgraph direction — it uses a single global direction ## Implementation Plan 1. **Layout** (fm-layout/src/lib.rs): In the Sugiyama layout, when processing nodes within a subgraph/cluster, check if the cluster has a direction override. If so, apply rank assignment and coordinate assignment with that direction for nodes within the cluster. 2. **Tests**: Create a flowchart with mixed directions (parent LR, child TB). Verify child nodes are laid out vertically within the horizontal parent flow. ## Complexity This is a non-trivial layout change. The current Sugiyama implementation uses a single global direction. Supporting per-subgraph direction requires partitioning the layout into independent sub-layouts and stitching them together. Consider a phased approach: first support the feature in the IR, then implement the layout logic.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.742818041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:08.413636558Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","layout","parity","parser"],"comments":[{"id":142,"issue_id":"bd-xwm3","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory) This bead MUST ship with: ### Unit Tests - Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases) - Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure) - Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values ### Integration Tests - Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs - Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs) - Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics ### E2E Validation Script Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Then the parser can store per-subgraph direction, and the layout can read it.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:08Z"}]} {"id":"bd-y2qs","title":"Implement class member visibility modifiers and type annotations","description":"# Class Member Visibility and Type Annotations ## What mermaid-js Supports \`\`\` classDiagram class Animal { +String name -int age #List~Food~ diet ~void breathe() +eat(Food food) bool +sleep()* : void } \`\`\` - \`+\` public, \`-\` private, \`#\` protected, \`~\` package - Type before name for attributes, after parentheses for return types - \`*\` suffix for abstract methods - \`$\` suffix for static methods ## Current State - Class blocks ARE parsed, member lines are captured - Visibility modifiers are NOT extracted — stored as raw text in member string - Type annotations are NOT parsed separately from names - No rendering of visibility symbols ## Implementation Plan 1. **Parser**: When parsing class block members, extract leading +/-/#/~ as visibility. Parse \`type name\` for attributes, \`name(params) returnType\` for methods. Detect */$ suffixes. 2. **IR**: Add IrClassMember { visibility: Visibility, name: String, return_type: Option<String>, parameters: Vec<String>, is_abstract: bool, is_static: bool }. 3. **SVG Renderer**: Render three-compartment class boxes: header (class name + stereotype), attributes section, methods section. Prepend visibility symbol. Show types. 4. **Tests**: All visibility markers, attribute types, method signatures, abstract/static.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:44.660117013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T09:33:44.181153568Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T09:33:44.180655345Z","close_reason":"Implemented class member visibility + three-compartment rendering. Parser already extracts +/-/#/~ visibility, types, static/abstract. Terminal renderer shows UML class boxes with compartment separators. SVG renderer shows three-compartment boxes with stereotypes, visibility symbols, type annotations, and separator lines.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["class","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":149,"issue_id":"bd-y2qs","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory) This bead MUST ship with: ### Unit Tests - Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases) - Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure) - Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values ### Integration Tests - Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs - Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs) - Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics ### E2E Validation Script Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason. ### Quality Gates All must pass before this bead can be closed: - cargo check --workspace --all-targets - cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings - cargo fmt --check - cargo test --workspace (zero new failures) ","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"}]} {"id":"bd-y6o8","title":"Implement style and linkStyle directive parsing and rendering","description":"# style / linkStyle Directive Support ## What mermaid-js Supports Inline styling for specific nodes and edges: \`\`\` style nodeId fill:#f9f,stroke:#333,stroke-width:4px linkStyle 0 stroke:#ff3,stroke-width:4px,color:red linkStyle default stroke:#333 \`\`\` style applies to a node by ID. linkStyle applies to an edge by index (0-based declaration order) or 'default' for all edges. ## Current State - Parser recognizes style/linkStyle lines but does not capture the property map - No rendering path for inline styles ## Implementation Plan 1. **Parser**: Parse \`style nodeId prop:value,...\` and store as IrNodeStyle on the IrNode. Parse \`linkStyle index prop:value,...\` and store as IrEdgeStyle on the IrEdge. 2. **IR**: Add \`inline_style: Option<IrInlineStyle>\` to IrNode and IrEdge. IrInlineStyle is a BTreeMap<String, String>. 3. **SVG Renderer**: Apply inline_style as SVG attributes (fill → rect fill, stroke → rect stroke, etc.). For edges, apply to <path> elements. 4. **Tests**: Verify style is applied in SVG output, verify linkStyle by index, verify default linkStyle. ## Considerations - Property names must be mapped from CSS-like (stroke-width) to SVG attributes - Sanitize values to prevent XSS in SVG output","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.486402415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23.800287285Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-y6o8","depends_on_id":"bd-77gh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:23.246993348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":140,"issue_id":"bd-y6o8","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory) This bead MUST ship with: ### Unit Tests - Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases) - Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure) - Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values ### Integration Tests - Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs - Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs) - Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics ### E2E Validation Script Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason. ### Quality Gates All must pass before this bead can be closed: - cargo check --workspace --all-targets - cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings - cargo fmt --check - cargo test --workspace (zero new failures) ","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:39Z"},{"id":185,"issue_id":"bd-y6o8","author":"ubuntu","text":"CROSS-DIAGRAM NOTE: style/linkStyle directives apply to flowchart primarily but the IR infrastructure should be generic enough for any diagram type. Also: style supports comma-separated node IDs: 'style nodeA,nodeB,nodeC fill:#f9f' applies to multiple nodes at once. The parser must split on commas and apply the style to each node.","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23Z"}]} {"id":"bd-yh4v","title":"Implement gantt date parsing, task types, and duration computation","description":"# Gantt Date/Duration/Task Type Parsing ## What mermaid-js Supports \`\`\` gantt dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD title Project Plan excludes weekends section Phase 1 Design :done, des1, 2024-01-01, 30d Implementation :active, impl1, after des1, 60d Testing :crit, test1, after impl1, 2024-05-01 Milestone :milestone, m1, after test1, 0d \`\`\` - dateFormat: YYYY-MM-DD, DD/MM/YYYY, etc. - Task types: done, active, crit (critical), milestone - Duration: Nd (days), after taskId (dependency), absolute dates - excludes: weekends, specific dates ## Current State - Parser recognizes sections and task lines, but does NOT extract dates, types, durations, or dependencies - Layout has a gantt algorithm but operates on generic nodes, not time-aware bars ## Implementation Plan 1. **Parser**: After the colon in task lines, parse comma-separated metadata: type (done/active/crit/milestone), id, start (date or 'after id'), end (date or duration). Parse dateFormat directive. Parse excludes. 2. **IR**: Add IrGanttMeta { date_format, excludes, sections: Vec<IrGanttSection> } with IrGanttTask { id, name, task_type, start, end, depends_on }. 3. **Layout**: Compute actual x-positions from dates. Apply excludes to skip weekends. Position milestone as diamonds. 4. **Tests**: Date formats, durations, after-dependencies, excludes, milestones, crit tasks.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:46:07.294100500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:31.708821785Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gantt","parity","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","depends_on_id":"bd-0bu2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:28.205838996Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":155,"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory) This bead MUST ship with: ### Unit Tests - Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases) - Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure) - Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values ### Integration Tests - Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs - Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs) - Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics ### E2E Validation Script Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason. ### Quality Gates All must pass before this bead can be closed: - cargo check --workspace --all-targets - cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings - cargo fmt --check - cargo test --workspace (zero new failures) ","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:41Z"},{"id":196,"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL GANTT DIRECTIVES TO PARSE: - todayMarker stroke-width:5px,stroke:#0f0,opacity:0.5 → custom styling for the today line. Store as optional style string in IrGanttMeta. - tickInterval 1day / 1week / 1month → controls axis label granularity. Store as enum (Day/Week/Month/Quarter/Year) in IrGanttMeta. - inclusiveEndDates → when true, task end dates include the end day. Store as bool in IrGanttMeta. - weekday monday → sets start of week. Store as optional u8 in IrGanttMeta. - axisFormat %Y-%m-%d → date format for axis labels. Store as String in IrGanttMeta. These must ALL be parsed for full parity. Update the IrGanttMeta type definition in bd-0bu2 accordingly.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:31Z"}]}
